mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers the Arctic region and its indigenous peoples and cultures (A, A.1, A.2), including territories of Norway, Russia, Canada, Alaska, Greenland, and Iceland; the sub-Arctic is geographically contiguous (B.1).
Covers the succession of indigenous Andean peoples and civilizations along the central and southern Andes (D.1).
Covers indigenous peoples of the Rocky Mountain region, relevant to the Northwest Coast (B.2) and Plateau (B.4) cultural areas.
Provides broad coverage of the entire North American region, encompassing all subregions (B.1-B.9) where indigenous peoples lived.
Covers Oceania including Australia (I.1), Melanesia (I.2), and Polynesia (I.3).
The Inca Empire was the dominant civilization of the central and southern Andes (D.1).
Colombia encompasses Andean, Amazonian, and Caribbean indigenous peoples, mapping to South American sub-regions (D, D.2) and Caribbean/Central American transition areas (C.3).
The Sahara spans North Africa including the Maghrib (F.1) and connects to the eastern Sudan (H.2) through the Sahel.
Directly covers Polynesian peoples, their Austronesian origins, and individual island groups (I.3).
Algeria is a core Maghrib country (F.1: northwestern Africa), with its Berber/Amazigh indigenous population and Arab-Islamic culture.
Turkey corresponds directly to outline path F.4 (In Turkey).
The Amazon rainforest is the primary locus of tropical forest peoples in South America (D.2).
The Andes is the geographic backbone of the central and southern Andes cultural area (D.1).
Swahili as language and culture is the primary marker of East African coastal and Great Lakes peoples (H.5, H.6).
Provides broad coverage of South American peoples and cultures (D), encompassing Andean civilizations (D.1), Amazon tropical forest peoples (D.2), and Southern Cone groups (D.3).
Covers sub-Saharan African history comprehensively (H), including Bantu expansion, Sahelian empires, and East African peoples.
Covers the peoples and cultures of East Asia (G.3), including Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and Mongolian cultural traditions.
Egypt corresponds to the Mashriq/northeastern Africa component of F.2.
Nigeria is the most populous country on the Guinea coast (H.3) and in the western Sudan zone (H.1).
Mesoamerica directly covers the peoples and cultures of Mexico, Belize, Guatemala, El Salvador (C.2).
Spans all indigenous American civilizations before 1492, covering North American (B), Middle American (C), and South American peoples (D).
Covers indigenous peoples globally across all continents and cultural regions.
The Atlantic slave trade drew primarily from West African coastal peoples (H.3: Guinea coast), the Congo basin (H.4), and the western Sudan (H.1).
Covers Pacific prehistoric migrations and European exploration of Oceanian island peoples (I), with relevance to Melanesian (I.2) and Polynesian (I.3) settlement.
